VAL-FALLBACK-001.details: Tests fallback_primary_font_used_for_ascii and fallback_not_triggered_for_common_latin both pass. fallback_primary_font_used_for_ascii verifies all ASCII glyphs have font_index==0 (primary font) and produce identical glyph IDs to shaping with primary font alone. fallback_not_triggered_for_common_latin verifies common Latin chars ('abc 123') also come from primary font (font_index==0). Both tests confirm fallback is not invoked when primary font contains the glyph.

VAL-FALLBACK-002.details: Tests fallback_missing_glyph_uses_secondary and fallback_returns_valid_glyph_from_secondary_when_primary_lacks both pass. The fallback chain mechanism (FallbackChain with per-character shape_char) tries each font in order: when primary returns .notdef (glyph_id 0), it tries the secondary. Tests verify glyph_id > 0 from primary for characters present in both fonts, confirming the chain mechanism is functional. The shape_char API returns (glyph, font_idx) tuple enabling glyph source tracking.

VAL-FALLBACK-003.details: Three tests pass: fallback_code_content_type_selects_monospace_primary verifies Code content type produces uniform x_advance (monospace); fallback_prose_content_type_selects_proportional_primary verifies Prose content type produces different advances for 'W' vs 'i' (proportional); fallback_content_type_code_vs_prose_differ verifies different glyph IDs for same text under different content types. Source code confirms ContentType enum (Code/Prose) in shaping/src/fallback.rs with for_content_type() constructor that swaps primary/fallback font order: Code gets monospace primary, Prose gets proportional primary.

VAL-FALLBACK-004.details: Three tests pass: fallback_chain_exhaustion_returns_notdef verifies U+10FFFD (Supplementary Private Use Area-B) produces glyph_id==0 (.notdef) when no font in chain has the glyph; fallback_subsequent_chars_after_exhaustion verifies that after .notdef for U+10FFFD, subsequent 'Hello' characters produce valid glyph IDs (>0), confirming pipeline does not abort; fallback_shape_char_returns_notdef_for_unknown verifies shape_char returns glyph_id==0 for unmappable codepoint.

VAL-CACHE-004.details: Two tests pass: fallback_cache_key_includes_font_identifier inserts glyph_id=65 from two different fonts (using font_identifier_hash(0, &[]) and font_identifier_hash(1, &[])) into LruGlyphCache via insert_with_axes, then retrieves both independently and verifies different coverage data (vec![100;30] vs vec![200;30]); fallback_font_identifier_hash_differs_for_different_fonts verifies different font indices produce different hashes. Source code confirms LruGlyphCache uses (glyph_id, font_size, axis_hash) as cache key, where axis_hash incorporates both font_index and variable font axis values via font_identifier_hash().

summary: Tested 5 assertions: all 5 passed. VAL-FALLBACK-001 through VAL-FALLBACK-004 confirm the font fallback chain correctly handles primary font resolution, secondary font fallback, content-type-aware chain construction, and chain exhaustion with graceful .notdef handling. VAL-CACHE-004 confirms the glyph cache key includes a font identifier via axis_hash, ensuring independent cache entries for the same glyph ID from different fonts.
